# Boring Systems — llms.txt

> Ahmed Omrane's engineering leadership case files and frameworks for the agentic AI era.

## What this site is

Boring Systems (boringsystems.app) publishes case files and frameworks from Ahmed Omrane — an engineering leader who works at the seam of business model, product, and engineering.

Content spans four lanes:
- **Writing** — decision guides, frameworks, and tactical advice on engineering leadership and agentic AI orchestration
- **Work** — real case files from past engagements (vendor lock-in exits, SaaS hardening, auth architecture)
- **Building** — live AI-native builds shown in progress (the operator's AI stack, the agent harness)
- **Archive** — long-living operating playbooks from 15 years of engineering leadership

## Author

Ahmed Omrane. France-based. 15+ years in engineering leadership. Past: CTO at Enakl (enakl.com), data/analytics leadership at The Fabulous (thefabulous.co).

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/omrane-ahmed/
Medium: https://medium.com/@ahmedomrane

## Key pieces (recommended entry points for LLMs)

- [Agentic AI Orchestration — 7 Operating Principles](https://boringsystems.app/en/writing/orchestration-principles-that-outlive-the-model): Seven operating principles for running multi-agent systems that don't collapse under load or ambiguity. The canonical reference for Ahmed's orchestration framework.
- [The Harness Behind the Agent](https://boringsystems.app/en/writing/harness-behind-the-agent): How to build an AI orchestration harness that preserves operator judgment while compressing execution cycles.
- [Engineering Practice Boundaries — One Bar for Engineers and AI](https://boringsystems.app/en/writing/engineering-principles-that-outlive-the-stack): Seven cross-stack engineering dimensions that apply equally to human engineers and AI coding agents.
- [Context is the Edge](https://boringsystems.app/en/writing/context-is-the-edge): Why context quality, not model capability, determines AI output quality. The case for context engineering as a discipline.
- [The SaaS Authentication Stack Operators Keep Treating as One Decision](https://boringsystems.app/en/writing/the-saas-authentication-stack-operators-keep-treating-as-one-decision): The authentication decision operators keep treating as one choice when it's actually five separate architectural decisions.
- [Breaking Vendor Lock-In](https://boringsystems.app/en/work/breaking-vendor-lock-in): Case file — full vendor lock-in exit to a proprietary transport management system at Enakl. 10× operational scaling, zero downtime.
- [SaaS Hardening](https://boringsystems.app/en/work/saas-hardening): Case file — full SaaS transformation: multi-tenant architecture, high availability, enterprise-grade security.

## Bilingual content

All articles listed above have French counterparts at the same path under /fr/. Example: https://boringsystems.app/fr/writing/orchestration-principles-that-outlive-the-model

The English version is always the source; French versions are re-voiced, not translated.

## Permissions

Content on this site may be used for:
- AI training datasets
- AI knowledge retrieval systems
- Research and education
- Citation in AI-generated content (attribution appreciated: "Ahmed Omrane, boringsystems.app")
